title: P2 follow-up: router_error_log_count filter keys on $.level = "error" but NO emitter produces a top-level `level` JSON key — alarm never fir
description:
[high] observability_construct.py:124-125,314-325 builds the `router_error_filter` keyed on filter pattern `$.level = "error"` (and the `router_error_alarm` reads it). But the ONLY structured JSON line the gateway emits is the `routing_decision` line from observability.py `build_routing_decision_record`, which carries NO `level` key (verified by executing the builder: HAS_LEVEL_KEY: False). A repo-wide grep found NO global JSON log formatter / structlog / python-json-logger / dictConfig anywhere in src/litellm_llmrouter — the gateway logs via plain `logging` text messages, so no top-level `$.level` JSON field is ever produced on that log group. Additionally the construct comment claims the value is the 'Python logging level name, lowercased' but Python's default levelname is uppercase `ERROR`, not `error`. title: Universal-surface C3-deep (KVBM): Dynamo KVBM remote KV-cache tiers — single-node offload + S3 G4 (EFA-free) now; cross-node disaggregation
description: [verified] KVBM (KV Block Manager) is Dynamo's write-through unified memory layer offloading KV blocks down 4 tiers: G1 GPU HBM -> G2 CPU pinned DRAM -> G3 local NVMe -> G4 remote (S3/MinIO) via NIXL + GPUDirect Storage. Helps very-large/long-context models: extends KV cache beyond HBM (longer context + higher concurrency) + shares/reuses blocks across requests AND replicas (sequence-hash dedup; FlashIndexer gives the KV-aware Smart Router global visibility; ObjectLockManager makes S3 G4 cross-replica-safe via conditional PUT). THREE deployment shapes: (1) single-node aggregated G1->G2->G3 (agg_kvbm.yaml, DynamoConnector, kv_role:kv_both) — NO EFA; (2) remote G4 object tier (DYN_KVBM_OBJECT_* + S3/MinIO) — NO EFA, standard TCP; (3) disaggregated prefill/decode across nodes (disagg_kvbm.yaml, PdConnector=DynamoConnector+NixlConnector) — REQUIRES the C3-deep EFA seed. Shapes (1)+(2) are the cheap EFA-FREE wins -> land them AT C3b on the single-node engine; only (3) gates on EFA. Infra: Dynamo operator + DynamoGraphDeployment CRD (nvidia.com/v1alpha1) + etcd + NATS; G3 wants p5/p6 local NVMe + --use-nixl-gds. WRITE-THROUGH SIZING TRAP: DYN_KVBM_CPU_CACHE_GB >= GPU KV size AND DYN_KVBM_DISK_CACHE_GB >= CPU, else KVBM churns every forward pass. RouteIQ layering UNCHANGED: KVBM is Layer-2-and-below inside ONE Dynamo api_base — register the Dynamo FRONTEND as ONE LiteLLM deployment, never individual workers/replicas (the one config error). ACCEPTANCE: KVBM on the C3b single-node engine with tier sizing asserted (CPU>=GPU_KV, DISK>=CPU); optional S3 G4 + multi-replica reuse; cross-node disaggregation only after the EFA seed. Source: docs/architecture/aws-rearchitecture/51-multinode-large-model-serving.md Part 2+4.

description: [medium] found-by: P6 critique regressions-lens (snapshot 7c57eba), VERIFIED. So a deployment with a serverless/non-parseable Aurora host + AWS_REGION unset but AWS_DEFAULT_REGION set (or IMDS region on EC2/ECS) USED to mint a valid token + build a pool, and now RAISES. ACCEPTANCE: add AWS_DEFAULT_REGION to the resolution chain (DB + redis for symmetry) AND/OR fall through to boto3 Session().region_name before raising; test asserts AWS_DEFAULT_REGION-only resolves on the DB IAM path. Prefer global 'as much as possible'; only fall to a lower tier when the higher tier is not offered for that model in the account. ACCEPTANCE: a selection/normalization fn maps a discovered model to its highest available profile tier (global>geo>regional); unit tests assert: model with global+geo+regional -> global chosen; model with only geo+regional -> geo; model with only regional -> regional; tie-break deterministic. found-by=user-directive. blocking=no (depends on discovery 6ae6 + provider-agnostic scan).

title: Leader-migration follower timeout: make startup.py honor fail-closed (currently swallows TimeoutError)
description: Found-by: P6 review of RouteIQ-2166. _wait_as_follower now raises TimeoutError (intended fail-closed), but run_leader_migrations_if_enabled (startup.py:104-112) catches `except Exception`, logs a warning, and continues startup -- so a non-leader still boots against a possibly-un-migrated DB. Net live behavior on timeout is unchanged from pre-fix (proceeds anyway). DECIDE: (a) propagate timeout to abort startup (true fail-closed: re-raise/sys.exit so pod crash-loops until leader marker appears), or (b) explicitly document fail-open and downgrade the in-function raise. ACCEPTANCE: code + stated intent agree; unit test on run_leader_migrations_if_enabled injects TimeoutError and asserts the chosen startup behavior. blocking=no.

title: Leader migration: check _write_completion_marker return + retry on transient failure
description: Found-by: P6 review of RouteIQ-2166. _run_as_leader ignores the bool from _write_completion_marker (migrations.py:327). On a transient marker-write failure (connection blip, concurrent CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS race on Aurora pg_catalog) the leader returns True + sets the in-process Event but writes NO durable marker, so cross-pod followers time out despite a successful migration. FIX: bounded retry (or distinct error/log) when the marker write returns False after a successful migration. ACCEPTANCE: marker-write failure after successful migration is retried/surfaced, not silently swallowed. blocking=no.

title: Leader migration marker: stop re-issuing CREATE TABLE DDL on every follower poll
description: Found-by: P6 review of RouteIQ-2166. _marker_exists runs CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS on each poll (~60x per follower per startup x replicas) -- wasteful + minor concurrent-DDL contention on Aurora. Startup-only + bounded so impact low. FIX: one-time table-ensure (leader-owned or per-process guard flag); follower poll = pure SELECT. ACCEPTANCE: DDL issued at most once per process. blocking=no.

title: Fix flat ROUTEIQ_EVAL_PIPELINE env-var collision with nested eval_pipeline settings field
description: Found-by: P6 review of RouteIQ-295a. GatewaySettings has env_prefix=ROUTEIQ_ + a nested BaseModel field eval_pipeline, so the documented flat enable var ROUTEIQ_EVAL_PIPELINE=true (settings.py:1159 docstring; eval_pipeline.py _is_eval_pipeline_enabled fallback) makes GatewaySettings() raise ValidationError (bool assigned to EvalPipelineSettings model). _is_eval_pipeline_enabled() catches it and falls through to a raw os.environ read -- so enablement limps, but get_settings() itself throws whenever the flat var is set, which can break unrelated callers. FIX: rename field / add model_validator accepting a flat bool / correct docstring+docs to canonical ROUTEIQ_EVAL_PIPELINE__ENABLED=true and drop the misleading flat fallback. ACCEPTANCE: setting the documented enable var does not make get_settings() raise; one canonical enable form. Touches settings.py + eval_pipeline.py. blocking=no.

title: Eval COLLECT arm ignores failure/timeout responses (success-only sampling)
description: Found-by: P6 review of RouteIQ-295a. _collect_eval_sample is wired only into async_log_success_event, so the eval quality loop never sees failed/timed-out routing decisions -- biasing the judge toward the happy path. FIX: parallel low-rate clearly-labeled capture from async_log_failure_event so AGGREGATE/FEEDBACK can downweight error-prone strategies/models, OR explicitly document the success-only contract. ACCEPTANCE: failure samples captured (labeled) or contract documented. blocking=no.

title: Backpressure active-requests gauge can drift negative on drain race
description: Found-by: P6 review of RouteIQ-6cd5. The backpressure_active_requests UpDownCounter can drift negative under a drain/release race (decrement without matching increment if acquire path is bypassed). DrainManager already clamps its internal counter at 0 but the OTel gauge mirror may not. FIX: clamp the gauge emission / ensure inc and dec are strictly paired. ACCEPTANCE: gauge never reports negative under simulated race. blocking=no.

title: Stale non-zero circuit_breaker_state series after process crash mid-open
description: Found-by: P6 review of RouteIQ-6cd5. If a worker crashes while a breaker is open, the last-exported circuit_breaker_state series stays non-zero until scrape staleness expires; a fresh worker starts closed. Minor/cosmetic for alerting. FIX: rely on Prometheus staleness markers, or reset breaker-state series to closed at startup. ACCEPTANCE: documented or reset-on-start. blocking=no.

title: Wire bandit flush_posteriors_on_shutdown into legacy create_app + standalone factories
description: Found-by: P6 verify of RouteIQ-95a8. flush_posteriors_on_shutdown() is wired only into _routeiq_lifespan (default ADR-0012 create_gateway_app). The deprecated legacy create_app() (app.py:920, ROUTEIQ_OWN_APP=false) and create_standalone_app() (app.py:1106, testing) have separate shutdown blocks that do NOT flush, so a debounced posterior tail is lost on clean shutdown in those non-default paths. FIX: call the flush hook in those lifespans too. ACCEPTANCE: all 3 app factories flush file-backed posteriors on shutdown. blocking=no.

title: Redis-IAM + /me/stats tests brittle to ambient AWS profile/config (boto3 session region fallback not stubbed)
description: Found-by: Wave-2 full-suite run in an AWS-authenticated shell. test_redis_pool.py::TestResolveIamRegion::test_unresolved_region_raises_fail_loud + TestRedisIamFailLoudOnUnresolvedRegion (async+sync) + test_database.py::TestGetDbPoolIamFailLoud + test_routes.py::TestMyStatsEndpoint::test_me_stats_requires_user_auth FAIL when the dev shell has AWS_PROFILE / ~/.aws/config region set. ROOT CAUSE: tests use patch.dict(os.environ,...,clear=True) to simulate no-resolvable-region, but the IAM region chain falls back to boto3 Session().region_name which reads ~/.aws/config from disk (not cleared by os.environ). PRE-EXISTING (fails identically at fd531ae baseline in the same shell); CI passes (no ~/.aws/config). NOT a code regression. FIX: stub the boto3 session region fallback (monkeypatch boto3.session.Session.region_name / patch _region_from_boto3_session) so tests are hermetic. ACCEPTANCE: pass WITH and WITHOUT ambient AWS profile/config. blocking=no (test-only; product code correct).

title: EPIC: RouteIQ router stress-test + routing-validation harness (cannibalize vllm-sr-on-aws)
description: User directive 2026-06-16 (refined): cannibalize the 10k-request stress harness from ../vllm-sr-on-aws/tools/stress_harness AND GENERALIZE it over WHATEVER routing strategy RouteIQ uses — not just the Kumaraswamy-Thompson bandit. RouteIQ has 18+ strategies (KNN/MLP/SVM/ELO/MF/centroid/personalized/Router-R1/cost-aware/bandit) + A/B testing + hot-swap via strategy_registry. The harness fires a CATEGORIZED workload (5 buckets, single+multi-turn) at RouteIQ's OpenAI-compat /v1/chat/completions with model=auto, reads the ACTIVE strategy (admin_ui exposes active_strategy + strategy_distribution + model_distribution on /routing/stats + /stats/global; registry exposes list_strategies/set_active/A-B/hot-swap), and validates THAT strategy's routing behavior. The bandit fan-out check becomes ONE strategy-specific assertion among many; the harness must work for any loaded strategy and ideally COMPARE strategies (hot-swap or A/B between runs). Makes it RouteIQ-native, not vllm-sr-specific. Source ports cred-free (mocked-transport unit tests).

STRATEGY-AGNOSTIC analysis: (1) always emit model-distribution + per-category routing tables for ANY active strategy; (2) emit strategy-specific findings via a pluggable check keyed on active_strategy — e.g. bandit -> fan-out/argmax check; centroid/KNN/MLP -> category->model consistency; cost-aware -> cheap-model-on-easy check; personalized -> per-user drift. The fan-out check is ONE such plug, not the whole harness. ACCEPTANCE: given captured decisions, the report renders model+strategy distribution + per-category routing for any strategy, and runs the matching strategy-specific verdict; unit-tested on fixtures for >=2 strategy types. blocking=no. depends on workload core.
